# Changelog

Release notes and upgrade guides for each DIAL platform (Helm stable-assembly) release, newest first. Each release links to its highlights and to the technical upgrade guide (component versions, configuration changes, and deprecations).

## DIAL 1.43

The highlights of this release include a new Evaluation Framework for AI quality assessment, expanded MCP ecosystem support with registry integration and application-level MCP endpoints, significant Quick Apps editor improvements, and expanded multimodal capabilities across Vertex and OpenAI adapters including video generation and audio support.

[Release notes](release-notes-1.43.md) · [Upgrade guide](upgrade-to-1.43.md)

## DIAL 1.42

The highlights of this release include significant improvements to MCP Toolsets including analytics and mid-chat authentication flow, additional chat UI enhancements for borderless attachments, Hugging Face registry integration, several new adapters, and initial support for Responses API. This release completes Q1 of our 2026 Roadmap.

[Release notes](release-notes-1.42.md) · [Upgrade guide](upgrade-to-1.42.md)

## DIAL 1.41

The highlights of this release include significant improvements to Quick Apps 2.0 including Agent Skills support, chat UI enhancements for custom applications and attachments handling, publication rules logic, and support for several new models.

[Release notes](release-notes-1.41.md) · [Upgrade guide](upgrade-to-1.41.md)

## DIAL 1.40

The highlights of this release include a total redesign of DIAL's File Manager, initial support for HuggingFace Model Servings in the DIAL Admin, quality-of-life enhancements to DIAL Chat including keyboard shortcuts, and major upgrades within DIAL's adapters.

[Release notes](release-notes-1.40.md) · [Upgrade guide](upgrade-to-1.40.md)

## DIAL 1.39

This month's release prioritized security- and performance-related tasks such as latency optimization and improved MCP toolset authentication functionality. The DIAL Admin Panel saw several enhancements such as the ability to import/export assets, and an integrated Deployment Manager component for deploying models and toolsets directly in DIAL. Several Llama and Gemini models were added to our adapters, as well as default configuration settings for Nano Banana. Feel free to watch a video showcase of some of the past month's biggest changes below:

[Release notes](release-notes-1.39.md) · [Upgrade guide](upgrade-to-1.39.md)

## DIAL 1.38

The highlights of this release are significant improvements to DIAL's Quick Apps functionality, multimodality enhancements with Sora and TTS/STT support, global interceptors, and improved feedback collection. Feel free to watch a video showcase of the past month's biggest changes below:

[Release notes](release-notes-1.38.md) · [Upgrade guide](upgrade-to-1.38.md)
